title: Define and use an interface
instruction:
An interface describes the shape of an object. Any object with the required properties satisfies the interface.

Run this.
successMessage: The object must have all required properties. Missing one or adding an unexpected type causes a compile error.

title: Optional properties
instruction:
Mark properties with `?` to make them optional. The object can omit them.

Run this.
successMessage: Optional properties may be `undefined`. The `??` (nullish coalescing) operator provides a fallback.

title: Functions in interfaces
instruction:
Interfaces can include method signatures. Any object implementing the interface must provide those methods.

Run this.
successMessage: The object satisfies the `Greeter` interface by providing both methods with matching signatures.

title: Extending interfaces
instruction:
Use `extends` to build more specific interfaces from general ones.

Run this.
successMessage: `Circle` inherits `color` and `area()` from `Shape` and adds `radius`. This is structural composition.
